Tests division of a Boolean object by false resulting in positive infinity.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion test that checks the behavior of JavaScript when dividing a `Boolean` object by a `false` literal. The expression `new Boolean(true) / false` is expected to evaluate to `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY`. This is because the `Boolean` object is coerced to the number `1`, and dividing by `false` (which is coerced to `0`) results in positive infinity. The test ensures that JavaScript's type coercion and arithmetic operations conform to expected behavior.
